title: pinus sacra
intro: Pinus sacra, also known as the Japanese red pine, is a species of pine tree native to Japan. The bark, needles, and cones of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for centuries, with the bark being particularly valued for its medicinal properties. The resin of Pinus sacra contains antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory compounds, which are believed to have a range of health benefits, including reducing fever and improving respiratory health. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as tea, oil, and extract, and is also used in traditional Japanese medicine, known as Kampo, to treat a variety of ailments.
